source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/GeneralizedMethodOfMoments.cs @ fc28c0c ; RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Models/UnivariateDistribution/Bulletin17CDistribution.cs @ fc28c0c -- EXACT EMITTER ORACLES (Task B12). The upstream GMM/B17C tests are STRUCTURAL-ONLY (no Estimate(), no numeric oracles), so these values are dumped from the REAL compiled RMC.BestFit GeneralizedMethodOfMoments/Bulletin17CDistribution via the dotnet oracle emitter (tools/oracle_emitter, un-excluding the three GMM/B17C files in B12) and reproduced by tools/verify_oracles.py. The C++/R/Python cores reproduce every value to ~1e-12 or better; tolerances follow fixtures/README.md's policy (optimizer-dependent parameters rel 1e-8; finite-difference-sensitive standard_error/covariance/correlation/quantile_variance rel 1e-7). This is a just-identified LP3 Bulletin17C GMM fit (q = p = 3), Iterative strategy, BFGS optimizer + numerical Jacobian, over exact-only annual_peaks. j_stat is ~0 for a just-identified fit (g(theta-hat) ~ 0): the exact residual is pure catastrophic-cancellation noise in g' V^-1 g (C# dumps +2.13e-06, the C++ core a differently-signed ~-5e-07), so it is NOT reproducible across compilers and is checked with an ABSOLUTE tolerance against 0, NOT pinned to either core's noise value. j_stat_pval is NaN because the degrees of freedom q - p = 0 (a just-identified LP3 fit cannot test its own specification). simulated_value rides this case: after the fit, the estimator's best parameters are pinned into the B17C parent and a seeded (sample_size=8, seed=20250705) ISimulatable stream is drawn -- the draws are the emitter's dumped stream, reproduced bit-close across C++/R/Python (identical compiled core) and to ~1e-13 against the real library.
